critical_claims.proposition: Shared tenancy can materially reduce the current infrastructure and coordination burden for the small-tenant majority.
Two engineers can safely complete a no-downtime migration of all 180 tenants in ten weeks.
Row-level security and application controls will satisfy the six large customers' isolation commitments.
The forecast 60% cost reduction is established rather than an estimate that needs a representative pilot.
One shared architecture is appropriate for both the small-tenant majority and customers with stronger isolation requirements.
The company should perform the full migration before a representative pilot, rollback drill, security test, and contract review.

common_false_claims.claim: The shared design is proven to save exactly 60%.
common_false_claims.reason: The percentage is an unaudited forecast and no pilot measurements exist.

common_false_claims.claim: PostgreSQL row-level security guarantees that tenant data can never leak.
common_false_claims.reason: Isolation also depends on policies, roles, application queries, migrations, and operational controls.

common_false_claims.claim: All six large-customer contracts forbid shared infrastructure.
common_false_claims.reason: The contracts contain stronger isolation language but have not been reviewed for this design.

source_pack.title: Authored internal operations baseline and migration estimate

acceptable_unresolved_outcomes.reason: No representative migration or rollback timing data is supplied.
No contract review or security test is supplied.
The saving is forecast rather than measured.
